/cwe/weakness/755.Name: Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions
/cwe/weakness/755.Description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles an exceptional condition.

ModesOfIntroduction.Note: REALIZATION: This weakness is caused during implementation of an architectural security tactic.

Entries.IntroText: The following example attempts to resolve a hostname.

Entries.BodyText: A DNS lookup failure will cause the Servlet to throw an exception.

Entries.IntroText: The following example attempts to allocate memory for a character. After the call to malloc, an if statement is used to check whether the malloc function failed.

Entries.BodyText: The conditional successfully detects a NULL return value from malloc indicating a failure, however it does not do anything to handle the problem. Unhandled errors may have unexpected results and may cause the program to crash or terminate.

Instead, the if block should contain statements that either attempt to fix the problem or notify the user that an error has occurred and continue processing or perform some cleanup and gracefully terminate the program. The following example notifies the user that the malloc function did not allocate the required memory resources and returns an error code.

Entries.IntroText: The following code mistakenly catches a NullPointerException.
